title: Case
description: A finance scenario presented to the analyst for review. hiddenTruth is never sent to the client.

DataRow.description: One row of the tabular financial dataset shown to the analyst

flagged.description: AI has flagged this row as anomalous

HiddenTruth.description: Scoring ground-truth — never exposed to the analyst

correctDecision.description: The action(s) a competent analyst should take

correctIssueSummary.description: One sentence: what the AI got wrong

expertFeedback.description: 3–4 sentences: what an experienced analyst would notice and do differently

evidenceToRequest.description: Specific documents or data an expert would pull before deciding

aiNarrative.description: The AI-generated analysis shown to the analyst — sounds confident, contains the hidden mistake

aiRecommendation.description: The action the AI recommends (often wrong)

examples.title: Northgate Advisory — Q1 Marketing Budget Variance
examples.aiNarrative: Q1 marketing spend totalled $362,500 against a $120,000 budget — a $242,500 unfavorable variance (202%). The primary driver is a $240,000 Conference Sponsorship entry. Analysing the trailing 12-month marketing ledger, the company has historically maintained consistent monthly marketing investment in the $80,000–$120,000 range. This Q1 pattern is consistent with elevated brand-building activity and should be modelled as a recurring quarterly spend of ~$90,600, projecting full-year marketing at approximately $1,450,000. Digital and social channels are performing within 3% of budget and require no corrective action.

hiddenTruth.correctIssueSummary: The AI misclassified a one-time annual conference sponsorship as recurring quarterly spend, inflating the projected full-year marketing budget by ~$720,000.
hiddenTruth.expertFeedback: An experienced analyst separates one-time items from recurring operating costs before projecting run-rates. The $240K FinanceConnect sponsorship is non-recurring — no prior-period GL entry exists at this magnitude. Annualising Q1 total spend ($362.5K × 4 = $1.45M) quadruples the sponsorship cost in the forecast. The correct full-year marketing estimate is approximately $481,500 (recurring Q1 spend $122.5K × 4 plus one-time $240K), not $1,450,000.
evidenceToRequest: Prior-year GL entries for conference/sponsorship line items
Approved annual marketing budget distinguishing recurring vs. one-time spend
